characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A half-Irish and half-Italian associate with the Lucchese crime family; he is the film's protagonist and narrator. Abled in the Adaptation: The real Henry had dyslexia so severe that it is said he didn't learn the alphabet until he was twenty and genuinely struggled in school, although his dyslexia and other issues wouldn't be discovered until after the film was released. The movie doesn't really touch on this fact, aside from him having a difficult time in school, and even then a viewer could easily get the impression that he did poorly simply because he was already uninterested in an honest living. Abusive Parents: His father beats him quite savagely early in the film. Granted he had just found out that Henry had skipped school for a month and was hanging out with hardened criminals but still. Adapted Out: His three year stint in the Army is never mentioned. Affably Evil: For an unrepentant gangbanger, Henry is consistently charming and laid-back. And is shown to have slightly more moral scruples compared to his fellow wiseguys.

characters.name: Henry Hill
characters.description: A half-Irish and half-Italian associate with the Lucchese crime family; he is the film's protagonist and narrator. Abled in the Adaptation: The real Henry had dyslexia so severe that it is said he didn't learn the alphabet until he was twenty and genuinely struggled in school, although his dyslexia and other issues wouldn't be discovered until after the film was released. The movie doesn't really touch on this fact, aside from him having a difficult time in school, and even then a viewer could easily get the impression that he did poorly simply because he was already uninterested in an honest living. Abusive Parents: His father beats him quite savagely early in the film. Granted he had just found out that Henry had skipped school for a month and was hanging out with hardened criminals but still. Adapted Out: His three year stint in the Army is never mentioned. Affably Evil: For an unrepentant gangbanger, Henry is consistently charming and laid-back. And is shown to have slightly more moral scruples compared to his fellow wiseguys.

characters.name: Jimmy "The Gent" Conway
characters.description: An Irish-American gangster and Lucchese associate; he is one of Henry's close friends. Actually Pretty Funny: When Spider tells Tommy to go fuck himself, Jimmy is actually delighted and amused that the kid stood up for himself and teases Tommy about letting the kid insult him. Unfortunately, this is too much for Tommy and he kills Spider on the spot. Adaptational Name Change: He was Jimmy Burke in real life. The filmmakers decided to usehis mother's maiden name of Conway, which he sometimes used as an alias,after his sister threatened to sue them. Affably Evil: An interesting case of someone who starts asAffably Evilbut gradually degrades toFaux Affably Evilwhen they begin to question loyalties. At first, he's very amiable and generous despite being a violent mobster. After the Lufthansa heist, he continues to act friendly even as his increasing paranoia drives him to kill anyone who threatens his plans. By the time Henry's trying to sell him silencers, he's not even pretending to be affable anymore. Almighty Janitor: While he's not even a made man (and is ineligible to become one by virtue of not being full-blooded Sicilian), he's a good enough earner to run his own crew of associates nevertheless and has enough power and respect that one could easily mistake him for an official capo.

characters.name: Paul "Paulie" Cicero
characters.description: A capo in the Lucchese family who takes Henry under his wing. Adaptational Name Change: He was Paul Vario in real life. Affably Evil: He's a very smooth and charismatic person and comes off as a father figure, especially to Henry. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Hates and refuses to own or use a phone, and dislikes large meetings, but is still a very capable capo. These idiosyncrasies help him avoid the cops, as the circle of people he tells things to is very small, and the cops have no phone to wiretap. Cigar Chomper: He puffs on large cigars through the film which perfectly fit his status as the man in charge.

characters.name: TommyDeVito
characters.description: A longtime associate of the Lucchese family and aspiring made man. Violent, hot-headed, petty, and willing to pull the trigger at the slightest provocation (or even for fun when drunk), his volatile temper and impulsive actions always unleash chaos. Adaptational Name Change: He was ThomasDeSimonein real life. Adaptational Ugliness: Joe Pesci isn't particularly bad looking, but the real Tommy was 6'2", had long hair and a mustache, was built like a boxer and as shown bythis photo◊could have been described as ruggedly handsome. Affably Evil: Quite possibly hisonlyredeeming quality. When he's in a good mood, Tommy can be very charming, entertaining, funny, and always ready for a joke, which makes him likable to some extent. However, that doesn't take away from the fact that he is also an extremely violent and dangerous mobster. It helps that, for all his faults, Tommy genuinely cares about his friends ("I didn't want to get blood on your floor") and is emphatically loyal to them in a life where loyalty and covering for each other is everything. Age Lift: In real life, Tommy was killed at age 28.Joe Pesciwas in his late 40s at the time this film was made, and it's suggested that TommyDeVitois also in his 30s since he's known Henry and Jimmy for at least a couple of decades.

characters.name: Karen Friedman Hill
characters.description: All Girls Want Bad Boys: As she puts it herself:Karen:I know there are women, like my best friends, who would have gotten out of there the minute their boyfriend gave them a gun to hide. But I didn't. I got to admit the truth. It turned me on. Berserk Button: Henry's infidelities. Finding out about his first infidelity has her go to his (first) mistresshouseto harass her, and put agun in her husband's faceasretribution. For contrast, she's much more lax with all of Henry's numerous other flaws, including his disappearing for days at a time, his cocaine addiction, his using her for a drug mule, and his intense selfishness. Brainy Brunette: She proves herself to be very smart throughout the film, like when she flushes drugs down toilet when the narcs are about to raid the house and when she leaves when it looks likeJimmy’s about to kill her Clingy Jealous Girl: Bordering onyanderelevels. Shepointed a gun in Henry's facewhen she found out he was cheating on her. Even Bad Women Love Their Mamas: Her biggest objection against being in witness protection is the prospect of never seeing her parents again. She's only swayed when the district attorney points out the mafia will potentially target her family to get to Henry and Karen, unless she joins him in the program.

characters.name: William "Billy Batts" Bentvena
characters.description: A Made Man in the Gambino crew who comes into conflict with Tommy. Adaptational Context Change: The real Billy Batts made the 'shinebox' joke in a club owned by Jimmy and was killed two weeks later at Henry's. The film compresses both events into the same night at Henry's club. Age Lift: The real Billy Batts was 37 when he was killed. In the movie, he's visibly graying and played by Frank Vincent who was age 53. Ain't Too Proud to Beg: His last words are weakly asking Tommy not to kill him. Alas, Poor Villain: Sure, he is a criminal himself, but seeing him brutally killed over such a petty reason....

characters.name: Morrie Kessler
characters.description: A Jewish associate who first tipped Jimmy off about the Lufthansa heist. Adaptational Name Change: He is based on Martin "Marty" Krugman. Bullying a Dragon: Morrie spends the entire movie pestering Jimmy and getting away with it. His luck runs out after fifteen years or so. Dodgy Toupee: His wig comes off when Jimmy beats him for his money. It's made even funnier by the fact that it happens just moments after one of his television ads is shown promising that his wigs never fall off. Five-Second Foreshadowing: Right before he is killed, he, Jimmy, Tommy, and Frankie enter a car. The latter three all shut their doors in unison, while Morrie lags behind with his, demonstrating that he is out of the loop with them.

characters.name: Frankie Carbone
characters.description: Dumb Muscle: As part of Jimmy's crew. He's not as close to Jimmy as Henry or Tommy, so he's usually relegated to doing grunt work. Flat Character: This is not due to lack of development by the writers, but specifically because Frankie is just that dumb. Human Popsicle: He's left hanging in a meat truck after getting whacked by Jimmy so the latter wouldn't have to pay him his share of the cash from the Lufthansa heist. The Pirates Who Don't Do Anything: He's never actually seen engaging in criminal activity, like stealing or committing violence. Mostly he's seen with the rest of Jimmy's crew at social gatherings, or he's accompanying Tommy as a driver or standing around as backup while Tommy actually does the gangster stuff. While he was directly involved in the Lufthansa heist, it's anOffscreen Moment of Awesomefor the crew. Skewed Priorities: He actually thinks he's supposed to hang around and make coffee as told to by Tommy while the latter offs "Stacks" Edwards. Likewise after Tommy kills Morrie, Frankie says he has to wait for the car to warm up before they can drive away despite Morrie's corpse being in full view in the front passenger seat. It's not explicitly stated, but Frankie's constant stupidity and poor judgment are clear reasons why Jimmy ends up having him whacked too.

characters.name: Stacks Edwards
characters.description: One of Jimmy's associates who helps pull off the Lufthansa heist. Boom, Headshot!: Shot in the head by Tommy from behind. Poor sap never saw it coming. Idiot Ball: Seriously, all he had to do was ditch the getaway truck in the junkyard and the crew would've been home free. But nope, the dummy parks it in plain viewnear a fire hydrantwhile he gets with a prostitute and gets high. The police eventually find it, ID the fingerprints on the steering wheel and start closing in on the group. Is it any wonder Jimmy had him killed? Small Role, Big Impact: He's just a background character, but it's his blunder, in contribution to the crew getting expensive items before things have cooled down, that forces Jimmy to kill him and near all the crew who participated in the Lufthansa heist. Token Minority: The only black man among the crew.

characters.name: Spider
characters.description: A hapless waiter who works at Jimmy's bar. Bullet Dancing: Subverted.When Tommy tries doing this to him, he gets shot in the foot. Butt-Monkey: Appears in two scenes, in one he is injured, in the other killed. Character Death: Killed by Tommy because he dared to talk back. No Name Given: It's not mentioned in the film, but Spider's name was apparently Michael Gianco.
